div.p: The President objects to stipulation proposed to be added by Lord Kimberley, as it implies a possible violation of agreement by United States. This Government can not consent to a modus which does not embrace paragraph 4 of proposition telegraphed by British ambassador. You can assure Lord Kimberley United States will enact legislation to enforce award on their part before August 1, and the President will not doubt Great Britain will do the same; but, if for any unexpected reason award should not be in force on that day, it is not desirable either party should have the right to denounce the treaty.
